title: DORA Post-Incident Review and Learning

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.13 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.13 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.10: DORA Post-Incident Review and Learning.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.10' running on notable macro, index=itsm (PIR/RCA records), dora_pir_completion.csv (KV store), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") status="Closed" earliest=-180d
| lookup dora_pir_completion.csv notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT pir_status, pir_date, root_cause, improvement_actions, actions_completed
| eval pir_due=if(isnull(pir_status), "MISSING", pir_status)
| eval actions_closed=if(isnotnull(actions_completed) AND actions_completed="yes", "DONE", "OPEN")
| stats count by pir_due, actions_closed
| append [
    search `notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") status="Closed" earliest=-180d
    | lookup dora_pir_completion.csv notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT pir_status, improvement_actions, actions_completed
    | where pir_status!="completed" OR isnull(pir_status) OR actions_completed!="yes"
    | table _time, rule_name, urgency, pir_status, improvement_actions, actions_completed
    | sort - _time
]
description: Article 13 requires financial entities to have capabilities and staff to learn from ICT-related incidents, share lessons, and evolve their ICT risk management framework. This use case tracks whether major incidents result in completed post-incident reviews (PIRs), that root causes are documented, and that improvement actions are implemented — providing the "learning and evolving" evidence DORA specifically mandates.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Create `dora_pir_completion.csv` KV store linking ES notable IDs to PIR records; (2) require PIR completion within 30 days of incident closure; (3) track root cause categories for trend analysis; (4) monitor improvement action implementation status; (5) report on learning cycle completion rates to management body; (6) share anonymised lessons across business units as Art. 13 requires. Indexes required: `index=itsm`.
visualization: Pie chart (PIR completion status), Table (incidents without PIR), Bar chart (root cause categories), Single value (PIR completion rate %), Timeline (PIR lifecycle). Key fields: `actions_closed`, `actions_completed`, `improvement_actions`, `pir_due`, `pir_status`, `rule_name`.
knownFalsePositives: Incidents during planned ICT changes, controlled DR drills, or after major upgrade waves.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` (PIR/RCA records), `dora_pir_completion.csv` (KV store).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Create `dora_pir_completion.csv` KV store linking ES notable IDs to PIR records; (2) require PIR completion within 30 days of incident closure; (3) track root cause categories for trend analysis; (4) monitor improvement action implementation status; (5) report on learning cycle completion rates to management body; (6) share anonymised lessons across business units as Art. 13 requires.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") status="Closed" earliest=-180d
| lookup dora_pir_completion.csv notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT pir_status, pir_date, root_cause, improvement_actions, actions_completed
| eval pir_due=if(isnull(pir_status), "MISSING", pir_status)
| eval actions_closed=if(isnotnull(actions_completed) AND actions_completed="yes", "DONE", "OPEN")
| stats count by pir_due, actions_closed
| append [
    search `notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") status="Closed" earliest=-180d
    | lookup dora_pir_completion.csv notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT pir_status, improvement_actions, actions_completed
    | where pir_status!="completed" OR isnull(pir_status) OR actions_completed!="yes"
    | table _time, rule_name, urgency, pir_status, improvement_actions, actions_completed
    | sort - _time
]
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Post-Incident Review and Learning (Art. 13)** — Article 13 requires financial entities to have capabilities and staff to learn from ICT-related incidents, share lessons, and evolve their ICT risk management framework. This use case tracks whether major incidents result in completed post-incident reviews (PIRs), that root causes are documented, and that improvement actions are implemented — providing the "learning and evolving" evidence DORA specifically mandates.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` (PIR/RCA records), `dora_pir_completion.csv` (KV store). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Invokes macro `notable` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pir_due** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **actions_closed** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by pir_due, actions_closed** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Appends rows from a subsearch with `append`.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare sample results with the ES Incident Review queue and analyst outcomes so counts, severities, and fields match what your security operations team triages in production.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Pie chart (PIR completion status), Table (incidents without PIR), Bar chart (root cause categories), Single value (PIR completion rate %), Timeline (PIR lifecycle).
grandmaExplanation: We stay on top of post-incident review and learning — so we can show we govern technology and resilience risk the way financial rules that apply to us require.
